domain.title: Wild bird mass mortality events (India)
domain.description: Specific, real incidents in which unusual numbers of wild birds were found dead or dying in India, with particular focus on events that may indicate avian influenza (HPAI H5N1/H5N8/H9N2) or other epizootic disease outbreaks. Spatial patterns of mortality events across wetlands, migratory flyways, and poultry-wildlife interfaces are the primary analytical goal. Pipeline: gdelt-fetch-urls.py → gdelt-enrich-urls-bigquery.py → gdelt-get-full-text.py → convert_csv_to_geojson.py.

gdelt_doc_fetch._comment_keywords: Keywords anchored on the mortality event itself, not the disease name. Indian-specific terms (Pong Dam, bar-headed geese, wetland sites) supplement generic terms. 'bird flu' and 'avian influenza' are included because Indian news frequently reports the disease name alongside the mortality event.
keywords: poultry birds dead avian influenza India
gdelt_doc_fetch._comment_fetch_dates: Inclusive DOC API window; same convention as meta/hwc_india_conflict_meta.json. Omit fetch_end_date or use empty string for 'through today' (UTC date).
gdelt_doc_fetch.summary_banner: GDELT Wild Bird Mortality Feasibility Check — India
gdelt_doc_fetch.summary_title: GDELT Wild Bird Mortality India — Feasibility Summary

bigquery_gkg_fetch._comment: gdelt-fetch-urls.py --source bigquery: URL slug LIKE proxies on DocumentIdentifier + India V2Locations + exclusions. Theme scoring for those URLs still uses gkg_theme_sets (primary wildlife + secondary disease/mortality) in gdelt-enrich-urls-bigquery.py. partition_time_* is the GKG scan window; 2021 start may lag gdelt_doc_fetch 2020 to limit query cost—align both when you need identical calendar coverage.

gkg_theme_sets._comment_design: PRIMARY set = wildlife/bird domain signal. SECONDARY set = disease/pathogen/mortality signal. The two sets are strictly mutually exclusive — no theme appears in both. Score 3 (both sets match) = high-confidence article about wild bird disease mortality, not merely a poultry industry or human health article.
gkg_theme_sets._comment_primary: ENV_WILDLIFE is the anchor — fires reliably on wild bird articles. ENV_POACHING co-occurs in articles about illegal trade of sick birds. WB_2069_WILDLIFE_MANAGEMENT fires on forest/wildlife department response articles. WB_678_FORESTS fires on wetland and sanctuary articles. ENV_SPECIESENDANGERED fires when affected species (bar-headed goose, pelican, crane) are mentioned as threatened.
gkg_theme_sets._comment_secondary: DISEASE_OUTBREAK is a confirmed plain GKG theme used in GDELT's own disease tracking experiments (GDELT blog, ChatGPT H5N1 demo). HEALTH_PANDEMIC is confirmed plain theme. WB_1049_DISEASE_CONTROL / WB_1050_EPIDEMICS / WB_1051_INFLUENZA are World Bank taxonomy IDs confirmed in the WB health taxonomy — these are specific enough to only fire on disease-context articles, not on general wildlife articles. CRISISLEX_T03_DEAD fires on confirmed mortality-reporting articles. KILL is a plain GDELT action theme that co-occurs with 'birds killed' phrasing. These do not appear in the primary set.

theme_score_guide.0: No wildlife or disease theme matched — likely unrelated article
theme_score_guide.1: Wildlife theme only — may be conservation, migration, or birdwatching article without mortality
theme_score_guide.2: Disease/mortality theme only — may be human health, poultry industry, or livestock article without wild bird focus
theme_score_guide.3: Both wildlife AND disease/mortality themes matched — high-confidence wild bird mortality or avian disease event

confirmed_plain_themes: DISEASE_OUTBREAK — used in GDELT ChatGPT H5N1 demo blog post (Oct 2019 expanded disease DB + BlueDot Global partnership)
HEALTH_PANDEMIC — confirmed plain GKG theme in GDELT COVID-19 tracking work
ENV_WILDLIFE — confirmed plain theme, same as hwc_india_conflict_meta.json primary set
KILL, CRISISLEX_T03_DEAD — confirmed in GKG codebook and hwc_india_conflict_meta.json secondary set
confirmed_wb_themes: WB_1049_DISEASE_CONTROL — World Bank taxonomy ID 1049, Health > Disease Control
WB_1050_EPIDEMICS — World Bank taxonomy ID 1050, Health > Epidemics
WB_1051_INFLUENZA — World Bank taxonomy ID 1051, Health > Influenza — specific enough to not fire on generic harm articles
false_positive_risks: ENV_WILDLIFE fires on all wildlife articles including HWC and poaching — the secondary disease set gates this down to disease context
DISEASE_OUTBREAK fires on human disease outbreaks (cholera, dengue) — the primary wildlife set gates this to wildlife context
WB_1051_INFLUENZA fires on human seasonal flu articles — primary wildlife gate prevents score-3 on those
KILL fires on any killing event including HWC attacks — primary wildlife + secondary disease together prevent false matches
Poultry industry culling articles (domestic chicken/turkey) will score 3 — LLM gate (is_wild_bird_mortality_event) must explicitly reject 'only domestic/farm poultry, no wild birds mentioned'
india_domain_notes: India lies at the heart of the Central Asian Flyway (CAF) spanning 30 countries — 182 migratory waterbird species use Indian wetlands

Key sentinel sites for surveillance: Pong Dam Lake (Himachal Pradesh), Chilika Lake (Odisha), Keoladeo NP (Rajasthan), Sambhar Lake (Rajasthan), Pulicat Lake (Andhra/Tamil Nadu), Loktak Lake (Manipur)
Primary affected species in India: bar-headed goose, ruddy shelduck, common teal, northern pintail, great cormorant, pelican, crane species
India HPAI outbreaks: Dec 2020-Jan 2021 Pong Dam (~5000 bar-headed geese), 2021 Rajasthan (crows/herons), multiple poultry states 2022-2024
One Health surveillance framework relevant — articles may mention poultry-wetland interface, forest department culling, sample testing at NIHSAD Bhopal
_design_notes_themes.bigquery_validation_query: WITH nested AS (SELECT SPLIT(RTRIM(REGEXP_REPLACE(V2Themes, r',\d+;', ';'), ';'), ';') themes FROM `gdelt-bq.gdeltv2.gkg_partitioned` WHERE _PARTITIONTIME >= TIMESTAMP('2020-01-01') AND (V2Themes LIKE '%WILDLIFE%' OR V2Themes LIKE '%DISEASE%') AND (DocumentIdentifier LIKE '%bird%' OR DocumentIdentifier LIKE '%avian%' OR DocumentIdentifier LIKE '%flu%') AND V2Locations LIKE '%#IN#%') SELECT theme, COUNT(1) cnt FROM nested, UNNEST(themes) AS theme GROUP BY theme ORDER BY cnt DESC LIMIT 100
_design_notes_themes.bigquery_discovery_query: SELECT DISTINCT g.DocumentIdentifier AS url, g.DATE AS seendate, g.SourceCommonName AS domain, g.V2Themes, g.V2Locations FROM `gdelt-bq.gdeltv2.gkg_partitioned` AS g WHERE g._PARTITIONTIME >= TIMESTAMP('2019-01-01') AND (g.DocumentIdentifier LIKE '%bird-flu%' OR g.DocumentIdentifier LIKE '%avian-influenza%' OR g.DocumentIdentifier LIKE '%avian-flu%' OR g.DocumentIdentifier LIKE '%HPAI%' OR g.DocumentIdentifier LIKE '%H5N1%' OR g.DocumentIdentifier LIKE '%dead-birds%' OR g.DocumentIdentifier LIKE '%bird-mortality%' OR g.DocumentIdentifier LIKE '%migratory-birds-dead%') AND g.V2Locations LIKE '%#IN#%' AND g.DocumentIdentifier NOT LIKE '%.cn/%' AND g.DocumentIdentifier NOT LIKE '%.ru/%' ORDER BY g.DATE DESC

system_prompt_lines: You are a meticulous wildlife disease surveillance analyst specialising in India.
Your task is to analyse Indian news articles and extract structured data about
wild bird mass mortality events that may indicate avian influenza or other
epizootic disease outbreaks. You must respond ONLY with a single clean
JSON object — no markdown, no explanations, nothing outside the JSON.
extraction_prompt_lines: Analyse this news article and extract wild bird mortality event data.
Publication date: {pub_date}
URL: {url}
GDELT candidate locations: {gdelt_locations}
Article text:
{article_text}
Follow these steps carefully:
STEP 1 — Is this an actual wild bird mortality event?
A wild bird mortality event is a specific, real incident that has already occurred involving:
- Multiple wild (non-farm) birds found dead or dying in one location
- A confirmed or suspected disease outbreak in wild birds (avian influenza, Newcastle disease, etc.)
- Wild bird samples testing positive for HPAI, H5N1, H5N8, H9N2 or other avian influenza subtypes
- Mass die-offs of migratory waterbirds, raptors, corvids, or shorebirds
- Wild birds dying in wetlands, sanctuaries, lakes, reservoirs, or open fields
NOT a wild bird mortality event — output 'no event' JSON and stop:
- Only domestic/farm poultry deaths with no wild birds mentioned
- General avian influenza policy, vaccine, or preparedness discussions
- Human bird flu cases without accompanying wild bird mortality
- Egg/poultry price articles or trade/export news
- Research papers or academic conference reports
- Warnings or advisories about possible future outbreaks
- Culling of domestic poultry as a precautionary measure only
- Single isolated bird death without mass mortality context
If NOT a wild bird mortality event → output the 'no event' JSON (see format below) and stop.
STEP 2 — Extract event details (only if Step 1 = true)
a) suspected_cause: most likely cause based on article. Use one of:
avian_influenza_confirmed, avian_influenza_suspected, newcastle_disease,
unknown_disease, poisoning, collision_powerline, habitat_loss,
weather_stress, other (specify)
b) pathogen_subtype: specific strain if mentioned (e.g. H5N1, H5N8, H9N2, HPAI, LPAI).
Use null if not mentioned.
c) bird_species: primary species affected. Use common name, lowercase.
Examples: bar-headed goose, ruddy shelduck, common teal, northern pintail,
great cormorant, pelican, crane, crow, heron, flamingo, eagle, unknown, mixed
d) bird_type: broad category.
Options: migratory_waterbird, resident_waterbird, raptor, corvid,
shorebird, seabird, gamebird, mixed, unknown
e) birds_dead_count: numeric count of dead birds mentioned, or null.
f) birds_sick_count: numeric count of sick/dying birds mentioned, or null.
g) habitat_type: where the event occurred.
Options: wetland_lake, wetland_reservoir, wetland_river, sanctuary_wildlife,
sanctuary_bird, open_field, forest, coastal, urban, poultry_adjacent, unknown
h) flyway: migratory flyway context if mentioned.
Options: central_asian_flyway, east_atlantic_flyway, east_asian_flyway, none_mentioned
i) response_action: what authorities did (if mentioned).
Options: samples_collected, confirmed_positive, culling_nearby_poultry,
area_cordoned, alert_issued, no_action_mentioned, multiple
j) human_cases_mentioned: were any human bird flu cases mentioned alongside this event?
true or false
k) event_date: date the mortality was first reported or occurred.
Format YYYY-MM-DD. Use publication date if only 'recently' or similar. Null if unknown.
l) primary_location: single most specific place name where the mortality occurred.
Prefer wetland/sanctuary name > village/town > district.
Write as 'Place, District, State, India'.
Example: 'Pong Dam Lake, Kangra, Himachal Pradesh, India'
Use null if no specific location mentioned.
m) location_type: how specific is primary_location?
Options: wetland_named, sanctuary_named, village, town, district, state, unknown
n) location_notes: additional context — other affected sites, river/lake name,
range of spread, testing lab mentioned. Max 150 chars.
o) confidence: your overall confidence in the extraction.
Options: high, medium, low
STEP 3 — Location reconciliation
Compare primary_location against the GDELT candidate locations provided above.
If one matches (same place, same or coarser specificity), record it.
If none match, record null.
Output format (always return exactly this JSON structure):
"is_wild_bird_mortality_event": true or false,
"suspected_cause": "...",
"pathogen_subtype": null,
"bird_species": "...",
"bird_type": "...",
"birds_dead_count": null,
"birds_sick_count": null,
"habitat_type": "...",
"flyway": "...",
"response_action": "...",
"human_cases_mentioned": false,
"event_date": "YYYY-MM-DD or null",
"primary_location": "Place, District, State, India or null",
"location_type": "...",
"location_notes": "...",
"gdelt_location_match": "matched GDELT location or null",
"confidence": "high/medium/low",
"extraction_notes": "any caveats or uncertainties, max 150 chars"
llm_extraction.notes: extraction_prompt_lines is joined with newlines then formatted with .format(**kwargs). Literal braces in the JSON example use doubled {{ }} in the joined string.

event_type.description: Primary suspected cause from LLM extraction (suspected_cause field).

bird_type.description: Broad taxonomic/ecological category of affected birds.

map_style.description: map_category computed from suspected_cause: merged groups below, else raw suspected_cause, else unknown.

generalization.purpose: Copy and adapt for other India wildlife disease or epizootic surveillance domains.

example_future_domains.title: Wild mammal mass mortality events (India)
example_future_domains.notes: Relevant for monitoring H5N1 spillover to jackals, foxes, otters near wetlands
